title: mirabilis laevis var. retrorsa
intro: Mirabilis laevis var. retrorsa, also known as the Hooked Water-hyssop or Hooked Marsh Mallow, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Mirabilis laevis var. retrorsa include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
